task: Add the next preview-only Pro Rust worker result commit intent gate: derive a non-writing worker-owned commit-intent payload from the existing result snapshot readiness/local replay path, expose a keyless API endpoint, render a compact graph-first web panel with idempotency and hosted gate blockers, and synchronize Pro docs/evidence. Keep this local-only and keyless: do not add persisted snapshots, real result-event writes, Postgres/Redis, hosted auth enforcement, real workers, provider execution, credential access, quota/billing mutation, OSS runtime changes, npm dependencies, package publishing, or live provider calls.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain the main tradeoffs so future maintainers can follow the change.
continuityRequirements: Mention reuse targets and any deliberate continuity break in evidence.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless preview-only worker result commit intent endpoint.
Pro web shell renders commit idempotency requirements, rejected write steps, and hosted blockers from the current run.
userVisibleEffects: The Pro graph-first shell can show the future worker-owned commit envelope and why it is still rejected.
riskJustification: This is a preview-only contract surface. It intentionally makes future worker-owned commit prerequisites visible without creating a write path or enabling hosted execution.
